name: @radzor/ab-test
description: Run A/B tests with deterministic variant assignment and conversion tracking. Supports multi-variant experiments, traffic allocation, and statistical significance calculation. Zero dependencies.

inputs.name: experiments
inputs.description: Array of experiment definitions with names, variants, and traffic allocation.

inputs.name: salt
inputs.description: Global salt for hash-based variant assignment. Change to re-randomize all assignments.

outputs.name: variantAssignment
outputs.description: Result of assigning a user to an experiment variant.

fields.name: inExperiment
fields.description: Whether the user is in the experiment (false if excluded by traffic allocation).

fields.name: totalConversions
fields.description: Total number of conversions across all variants.

fields.name: significant
fields.description: Whether the result is statistically significant (p < 0.05).

events.name: onConversion
events.description: Fired when a conversion is tracked for an experiment.

actions.name: getVariant

params.name: experimentId
params.description: Experiment to assign the user to.

params.name: userId
params.description: Unique user identifier for deterministic assignment.

actions.description: Deterministically assign a user to a variant. Same userId always gets the same variant.

actions.name: trackConversion
actions.description: Record a conversion event for a user in an experiment.

actions.name: createExperiment

params.name: trafficPercent
params.description: Percentage of users included in the experiment (0-100).

actions.description: Define a new experiment at runtime.

actions.name: getResults
actions.description: Get aggregated results with conversion rates and statistical significance.

connectsTo.description: Track conversion events in analytics pipeline

llm.constraints: Variant assignment is deterministic using a hash of (salt + experimentId + userId). All data is in-memory — results reset on restart. For persistent experiments, store assignments and conversions in a database. Statistical significance uses a chi-squared approximation and requires at least 30 participants per variant.
